financial.reason: Sum assured of Rs. 1,00,00,000 against annual income of Rs. 12,00,000 gives a coverage ratio of 8.3x, well within the 20x maximum permitted for the 41-50 age band.
Monthly EMI of Rs. 35,000 against net salary of Rs. 85,000 gives a ratio of 41.2%, which falls in the 40%-55% medium-risk band, indicating moderate financial stress.
CIBIL score of 705 falls in the 650-749 'good' band — acceptable, but below the 750+ threshold considered low risk.

medical.reason: Systolic of 128 is within the low-risk range, but diastolic of 82 exceeds the 80 threshold, placing the reading in the medium-risk band (130-145 / 80-90) for age 18-55.
Total cholesterol of 210 mg/dL falls within the 200-239 mg/dL medium-risk band.
BMI of 24.5 falls in the 23.0-27.4 medium-risk band per the Indian population BMI chart.
Type II diabetes, diagnosed and on treatment, with HbA1c of 6.8% — below the 7.0% threshold, classified as well-controlled / medium risk.

medical.value: Father MI at 58; sibling heart disease
medical.reason: Father died of a heart attack at age 58 (before age 60), and a sibling also has heart disease — multiple early cardiac events in first-degree relatives escalate this to high risk.
Creatinine of 1.1 mg/dL and eGFR of 95 are both within the normal / low-risk range.
AST/ALT levels are within 1x the normal range, indicating no liver function concern.
Normal sinus rhythm at 74 bpm, within the 60-100 bpm low-risk range, with no arrhythmia or conduction abnormality detected.
TSH of 2.5 mIU/L falls within the 0.5-4.0 mIU/L normal range.

lifestyle.reason: Software Engineer is an office-based, desk-based role with no physical hazard exposure — classified low risk.

lifestyle.value: No hazardous avocations or adventure activities declared
lifestyle.reason: Applicant did not declare participation in scuba diving, mountaineering, aviation, parachuting, hang gliding, ballooning, or motorsports — no avocation risk to assess.
Applicant is a declared non-smoker with no nicotine use, placing them in the lowest risk tier.
Applicant reports occasional alcohol use at roughly 2 units/week, which falls in the 1-2 times per week medium-risk band.
Zero drunk driving incidents recorded in the last 12 months.
No driving accidents recorded in the last 12 months, within the 0-accidents low-risk band.

Age calculated from date of birth matches exactly across the application form and ITR — within the 1-year tolerance for rounding.
